title: veronica lutea
intro: Veronica lutea, also known as the golden speedwell, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and square stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Veronica lutea include its ability to relieve coughs and congestion, soothe irritated throats, and help to reduce fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular remedy in traditional folk medicine.
